project_name: Rolling River Pokemon AR Catcher (RRDC GO)
design_philosophy: Camp-y, adventurous, kid-friendly but not babyish. High-energy tactile UI with spatial/AR elements. The design blends 'Organic & Earthy' nature motifs with 'Vibrant Play' energy. Prioritizes thumb-free AR interactions, depth layering, and tactile, chunky UI elements suitable for iPad displays.

components.buttons: Chunky, tactile Neumorphism variant. Use rounded-2xl, vibrant colors, and a solid 4px bottom border/shadow to simulate physical depth. Active state translates down and removes the shadow.
components.cards: Bento-style grids. rounded-3xl with generous p-6 padding. Soft subtle borders (border-slate-200) instead of heavy shadows for a clean look.
components.glass_panels: For AR Overlays (Catch Screen) and floating navs: backdrop-blur-xl bg-white/60 (or black/40 for contrast) with 1px solid border-white/40 inner stroke. Ensures camera remains visible while text stays readable.
components.badges: Rarity badges must be pills (rounded-full) with bold uppercase text. Legendary badge gets an animated subtle pulse or glow.

motion_and_interactions.pokemon_idle: Continuous, smooth vertical bobbing via Math.sin() in Three.js useFrame. Optional slight rotation around Y axis.
motion_and_interactions.ball_throw: Swipe up gesture triggers a parabolic arc animation. The ball scales down as it moves 'away' into the Z-axis, simulating depth.
motion_and_interactions.modals: Framer Motion spring animation: initial={{ scale: 0.8, opacity: 0 }} animate={{ scale: 1, opacity: 1 }} transition={{ type: 'spring', bounce: 0.5 }}.
motion_and_interactions.hover_states: Cards lift slightly (-translate-y-1) and gain shadow. Buttons visually depress.

login_bg.description: Vibrant cartoon landscape of the camp. Used as a full-cover background for the Login Screen.

river_ball.description: The Rolling River Pokeball. Used in the AR Catch screen at the bottom center.

pokemon_otter.description: Camp Otter mascot. Use as one of the pre-seeded Pokemon or placeholder.

pokemon_pinecone.description: Pinecone creature. Use as one of the pre-seeded Pokemon or placeholder.

login.layout: Fullscreen background (login_bg). Center the login form in a glassmorphic card (Crystal Glass pattern).
login.elements: Camp Logo/Title at the top (H1, Fredoka font). Chunky login button (primary blue).

ar_catch.layout: Absolute full-screen video element for live camera feed. Z-index 10 is the Three.js Canvas overlaying the video. Z-index 20 is the UI.
ar_catch.three_js: Use textured PlaneGeometry for Pokemon images. Ensure planes always face the camera (billboarding). Implement a floating/bobbing animation.
ar_catch.ui_overlay: Top-left: 'Flee/Back' glass pill. Top-right: User stats/Group glass pill. Bottom-center: The river_ball image, wrapped in a Framer Motion div or gesture handler for the swipe-to-throw mechanic.
ar_catch.catch_modal: Appears on hit. Shows Pokemon image, name, randomly rolled power level (1-1000), and rarity badge. Must clearly state 'Caught by [Username]'. 'Go to My Collection' button.

collection_bank.layout: Scrollable grid view. iPad optimized (grid-cols-3 or grid-cols-4).
collection_bank.elements: Header with 'My Collection' (camp-themed). Filter/Sort dropdowns or pill toggles. Empty state: playful message indicating no catches yet.
collection_bank.card_design: Image takes up 60% of card, soft background color based on rarity. Bottom 40% shows Name, Power, and Rarity badge.

director_panel.layout: Admin dashboard. Sidebar on the left (Desktop/iPad Landscape) or top-nav (Portrait).
director_panel.elements: Stats overview cards (Total Catches, Active Users). Tables for User Management and Pokemon Seed Editor. Toggle for Global Spawns.
director_panel.style: More utilitarian than the game screens, but retains the camp colors and Fredoka headings.

instructions_to_main_agent: Write all React code in .jsx or .js files as this environment does not use TypeScript.
In tailwind.config.js, extend the theme to include the custom fonts (Fredoka, Nunito) and custom colors defined in this JSON.
Add 'import_url' from typography section to your global index.css or inject it via a link tag in the HTML head.
For the AR feature, use standard HTML5 <video> element with navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia() positioned absolutely behind a Three.js Canvas.

For the Pokemon 3D rendering, use @react-three/fiber and @react-three/drei. Render the Pokemon as a textured plane using Drei's <Image> or <mesh> with a texture. Animate its position.y in a useFrame loop.

CRITICAL TESTABILITY: All interactive elements (buttons, inputs, filters, specific cards) and key informational elements (power levels, rarity text) MUST include a data-testid attribute formatted in kebab-case based on role (e.g., data-testid='login-submit-button', data-testid='pokemon-card-otter').
Use standard Shadcn components for the Admin Panel, but heavily customize their border-radius and spacing to match the 'tactile' camp aesthetic.
Ensure 'river_ball' logic uses simple drag/swipe logic (e.g. framer-motion drag or react-use-gesture) and calculates intersection with the 3D plane.
Use framer-motion for UI transitions, especially the Catch Success modal popping up over the camera.
